name: Quentin Jackson
abstract: Quentin "Butter" Jackson (January 13, 1909 – October 2, 1976) was an American jazz trombonist.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Quentin Jackson just left Duke Ellington... We worked with Nat Cole... I took the plane to Paris... and Billy Byers. We went to Paris... Got in there. Did 13 gigs there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Quentin Jackson, everyone knows as 'Butter', was a trombone player with Zach. (End of side 1) ... Roy and Vic and I -- Zach had a great pianist, Herman Chittison

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Finally I found out that Quentin Jackson – Butter, we called him – was a good buddy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I was on with the band, Quentin Jackson, Butter as we called him

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: the first time I met Big Sid Catlett was in Miami in 1935; he came there with Don Redman, and in that band was Denny Morton, Quentin Jackson was the other trombone player

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Quentin Jackson in the context of a band, but the interviewee does not respond with any personal connection or confirmation of meeting him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995
